dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 5 at Motel Room 105 for a reported GSW to the lower abdomen.
Hi, my name is Luis, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
Where are you experiencing the most pain or discomfort?
I got shot in the side. It's really painful.
Can you tell me your name, please?
Yeah, Abraham. Abraham DeCesare.
Mr. DeCesare, can you tell me the name of this city and where you are now?
I'm in a motel. In [CITY]. Room 105.
And can you tell me the date and time now, Mr. DeCesare?
Feb 17, around 8 o'clock.
Can you tell me what made you call 911?
I got shot in the hip area from a close range. I'm in a lot of pain.
Can you speak clearly and without difficulty, Mr. DeCesare?
Yeah, it doesn't affect my throat or mouth.
Your airway is patent and your voice sounds clear.
Are you having any trouble breathing or feeling short of breath?
Respirations are 16 per minute, even, and without labored effort.
Oxygen saturation is at 100% on room air.
Lung sounds are clear to auscultation in all fields-clear to auscultation.
May we assess your pulse and blood pressure now, Mr. DeCesare?
Heart rate is 69, strong, and regular in rhythm.
Blood pressure is 123 over 74, with a normal finding in early trauma cases.
Mr. DeCesare, with your hand, can you squeeze mine tightly?
Yep, I can.
Any signs of shock or injury symptoms you're aware of?
I'm pale and sweating a lot. I think I'm losing strength on my left side.
Skin is cool, pale, and definitely diaphoretic.
Also, pedal pulse on the right leg is strong, but the left is weak.
Can you describe how the shooting happened?
He was outside when a man approached with a gun. He argued and then was shot in the lower abdomen.
Mr. DeCesare, so you were trying to protect someone when this happened?
Yes. My girlfriend was being threatened, so I stepped in. He shot me at close range.
Was the shot from a gun or something else?
Gun. I think it was a small caliber, from close range.
Was there any struggle after you were shot?
I heard one shot and then fell. The man ran away afterwards.
Did anyone assist you after the shooting?
My girlfriend helped bring him inside and called an ambulance.
Right after the incident, how did you feel and what were your symptoms?
Extremely painful. A pressure in my side and I couldn't move well.
On a scale from 1 to 10, how severe is your pain?
It's 10-the absolutely worst.
Does the pain move to any other area-like your back or chest?
It radiates to the back and into the pelvis region.
Let's do a quick visual assessment of your skin. How do you feel your hands and feet now?
My hands and feet feel cold, but no numbness or tingling.
Your abdomen appears to be distended and tender. May we check in more detail?
Wound is in the left lower quadrant with minimal bleeding. There is tenderness with palpation.
No signs of gross contamination or puncture beyond the entrance wound.
Pulse is currently 69, steady and regular.
Respiratory rate is 16 per minute, with even and normal depth.
Blood pressure is 123 over 74. Stable for now.
Oxygen saturation reads 100% on room air. A&O x4, showing full awareness of time, place, person and events.
Glucose is not available in the field to take a reading.
EKG is showing a sinus rhythm with no acute changes at the moment-ECG showing sinus rhythm-normal baseline.
We're going to insert IV access on both arms, just to be ready with fluids if needed.
I don't need oxygen or anything else-just take me to the hospital.
Understood. Two IV lines will be running-normal saline in one and a saline lock in the other.
IV in the right arm is starting with 500 mL Normal Saline.
IV is placed in the left arm - this will remain a saline lock for backup.
EKG is up and running-monitoring baseline with no acute issues.
Following protocol 4-2 for abdominal trauma. You'll remain alert and under close monitoring.
At seven minutes in, heart rate is at 72.
Respirations still comfortable-16 per minute.
Blood pressure has dropped slightly to 110 over 61.
Oxygen saturation has declined to 87%.
EKG still reads sinus rhythm with no acute changes. No chest pain or dyspnea at this time.
Mr. DeCesare, is the pain still 10/10 like before?
It's 10 and constant. No improvement.
Does any activity or deep breathing make the pain worse?
Any motion or deep breath worsens the pain.
Did you take any medications before the incident?
No. I don't take medications or any drugs.
Mr. DeCesare, [COMPANY_NAME] is the preferred facility for penetrating trauma, but we can take you to [CITY].
Yeah, I want to go to [CITY]. I've been there before. Go as fast as you can.
We'll keep you in semi-Fowler's position to improve breathing and comfort.
The stretcher is locked and secure. IV lines are in good condition and monitored.
We'll use lights and sirens for a fast response. Updates during the ride will be provided.
Traffic is expecting delays, but your transport is prioritized. [CITY] has been alerted.
Rechecking IV-fluids flowing, no signs of disconnection.
Status stable-72 heart rate, 110/61 BP, EKG normal with stable rhythm.
EKG still clear. We'll continue checking every five minutes.
Through the light and onto the main road now. Expecting a smooth ride from here.
Do you have any difficulty moving your limbs or back at all?
It's hard to move. The pain is so bad that I'm barely using the left side. I can feel my feet and hands, though no strength there.
Have you observed any changes in your urine or stool, or any rectal bleeding?
I don't feel like I have to go. No bleeding that I know of, and I'm just focused on the pain here.
Let's evaluate your neck. I note that your neck veins appear normal, and no pain present when moving it left to right or up and down.
[CITY] is the destination. IVs secure and monitored. We're on a smooth road now. How are you feeling?
It still hurts, but I'm okay as long as you get me there fast. I don't feel lightheaded or sick to my stomach, just very sore here and a bit tired.
EKG is still showing sinus rhythm with no concerning findings, and IV lines are working well and secured. It looks good from my side.
I just fired up my headlights again. The ER team is ready for us on arrival. We'll be there in about 6 minutes. You're doing well so far, sir-keep breathing steady for us.
Trauma team at [CITY] has been advised. Major penetrating wound to the left lower abdomen, GSW. Abdominal distension noted with strong pedal pulse in RLE and weak LLE pulse.
A&O x4, showing full awareness. Two large IVs in place, patient with no glucose reading available-under trauma observation. Wound minimal bleeding but distension likely.
Clear to auscultation in lung fields and clear rhythm with EKG-ECG-sinus rhythm. Oxygen level currently at 87%.
